articles.title: CHARACTER.AI LAUNCHES WORLD'S FIRST AI-NATIVE SOCIAL FEED
articles.summary: Character.AI's Feed is the world's first AI-native social platform featuring scrollable content with Characters, Scenes, and Streams. The platform transforms passive consumption into interactive creation, allowing users to remix storylines and build on others' content. Feed is rolling out on the mobile app with multimodal features including Chat Snippets, Character Cards, and Avatar FX video creation.

articles.title: INSTAGRAM LAUNCHES REPOSTS AND COPIES SNAP MAPS IDEA
articles.summary: Meta is adding Instagram Map and post reposting to make Instagram more social and focused on real connections, shifting away from its video-heavy, algorithm-driven feed. While Instagram Map shares user location only with consent and limited updates, and post reposting boosts friends' content in the main feed, some users have raised privacy concerns, despite Meta's emphasis on user control.

articles.title: TINDER EXPLORES A REDESIGN, DATING ‘MODES,' AND COLLEGE-SPECIFIC FEATURES TO BOOST ENGAGEMENT
articles.summary: Tinder faces declining user engagement with a 7% drop in paying users, prompting the company to introduce new features, including "modes" for different dating goals and college-specific options. The dating app is planning a broader redesign targeting Gen Z users that will feature AI-powered matching expansion and allow users to like specific profile parts. Despite flat revenue of $864 million in Q2, Match Group expects improved Q3 performance and announced $50 million in product development investments.

articles.title: DIGITAL TWINS: SIMULATING HUMANS WITH GENERATIVE AI
articles.summary: Digital twins are generative AI models that simulate specific individuals to predict their preferences and behaviors in new situations. These AI proxies can fill survey gaps, enable shorter studies, and forecast population trends by aggregating individual predictions. While showing promise for research efficiency, digital twins raise significant ethical concerns about consent, privacy, and misrepresentation.

articles.title: UX DESIGNER SKILLS: WHAT HIRING MANAGERS ACTUALLY LOOK FOR IN 2025
articles.summary: UX hiring managers in 2025 prioritize strategic thinking and problem-solving over polished interfaces and tool proficiency. Essential skills include user research, data analysis, AI literacy, accessibility knowledge, and strong collaboration abilities. Portfolios should demonstrate real impact through case studies with clear decision-making processes and measurable outcomes.

articles.title: THIS MACOS 26 ICON STRATEGY PUNISHES MAC USERS MORE THAN DEVELOPERS
articles.summary: macOS 26 has sparked backlash over design changes, particularly the revised Finder and Macintosh HD icons, with users criticizing inconsistencies in perspective, added visual clutter, and a lack of clarity. Apple's new layered glass icon style and enforcement of a uniform squircle shape—often resulting in blurry visuals and “gray box” placeholders for non-conforming third-party apps—has led to usability concerns and a perception that the Mac is becoming harder to navigate.

articles.title: BUILD NATIVE MOBILE APPS, FAST
articles.summary: Rork builds complete, cross-platform mobile apps using AI and React Native.

articles.title: PHOTO AND VIDEO AI EDITORS
articles.summary: Polarr offers applications to allow creators to make and distribute photos and video filters.

articles.title: BUSINESS NAME GENERATOR
articles.summary: Namelix uses generative AI to create a short, brandable business name.

articles.title: DO LLMS IDENTIFY FONTS?
articles.summary: A developer created a live benchmark using font identification requests from the dafont.com forum to evaluate how well LLMs like GPT-4o-mini and Gemini 2.5 can recognize fonts in previously unseen images. Despite a clever setup to avoid benchmark contamination, the models performed poorly, showing how LLMs still struggle with certain real-world visual classification tasks.

articles.title: NIHILO TAKES ON THE RUM CATEGORY WITH THEIR OWN PREMIUM BRAND, ESTHER
articles.summary: Nihilo created Esther as a bold reimagining of rum. Using optical illusions, duality, and unexpected design choices to challenge traditional category clichés, the brand was built through radical transparency and strategic design with the aim of making rum culturally relevant and widely appealing.

articles.title: WALK A THOUGHT'S IDENTITY FOR A FLAVOURED SYRUPS BRAND IS SWEET AND STYLISH
articles.summary: Bouquet, a whimsical and nostalgic brand of infused syrups by Caroline Lafleur, was brought to life through a playful and functional identity by French studio Walk a Thought, which blended warm European café imagery with vibrant visual storytelling. From unique typography and flavor-specific stickers to charming illustrations and a syrup-soaked website experience, the branding makes each bottle both memorable and practical for busy baristas and mixologists.

articles.title: CREATIVE DESIGN OBJECTS TURN FIRST MEETINGS INTO MEANINGFUL CONNECTIONS
articles.summary: The tok_u project transforms awkward first meetings through four thoughtfully designed objects that encourage genuine connection and interaction.

articles.title: IOS 26 BETA ADDS A CUTE NEW LOW BATTERY ANIMATION
articles.summary: Apple's iOS 26 beta 5 introduced several visual updates, including a new radar-like red circle animation for low battery notifications and refreshed splash screens for apps like Apple Music and Reminders.

articles.title: THE DESIGNER INVESTOR ERA
articles.summary: Figma's IPO represents the largest liquidity event for product designers in history, creating a new cohort with capital.
